perturbation.original_text: The above restrictions on the use or disclosure of the Confidential Information shall not apply to any Confidential Information that: (a) is independently developed by Receiving Party without reference to the Confidential Information, or is lawfully received free of restriction from a third party having the right to furnish such Confidential Information; (b) has become generally available to the public without breach of this Agreement by Receiving Party; (c) at the time of disclosure to Receiving Party was known to such party free of restriction; or (d) Disclosing Party agrees in writing is free of such restrictions.
perturbation.changed_text: The above restrictions on the use or disclosure of the Confidential Information shall not apply to any Confidential Information that: (a) is independently developed by Receiving Party without reference to the Confidential Information, or is lawfully received free of restriction from a third party having the right to furnish such Confidential Information; (b) has become generally available to the public without breach of this Agreement by Receiving Party; (c) at the time of disclosure to Receiving Party was known to such party free of restriction; or (d) Disclosing Party agrees in writing is free of such restrictions. **However, any collection of individual pieces of information, each of which might meet one of the exceptions above, will still be considered Confidential Information if the aggregate of the information creates a new insight or understanding not previously known.**
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a contradiction. Subsections (a), (b), (c), and (d) explicitly state that information meeting those criteria is *not* subject to the restrictions. By adding the caveat that a *collection* of such information *is* restricted if it creates a new insight, we are now saying that information meeting criteria (a)-(d) *can* still be considered Confidential Information, directly contradicting the initial statement of Section 4.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The above restrictions on the use or disclosure of the Confidential Information shall not apply to any Confidential Information that: (a) is independently developed by Receiving Party without reference to the Confidential Information, or is lawfully received free of restriction from a third party having the right to furnish such Confidential Information; (b) has become generally available to the public without breach of this Agreement by Receiving Party; (c) at the time of disclosure to Receiving Party was known to such party free of restriction; or (d) Disclosing Party agrees in writing is free of such restrictions. However, any collection of individual pieces of information, each of which might meet one of the exceptions above, will still be considered Confidential Information if the aggregate of the information creates a new insight or understanding not previously known.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that information meeting conditions (a)-(d) was NOT subject to restrictions. The changed text adds a caveat stating that a *collection* of such information *IS* restricted under certain conditions, which directly contradicts the original statement that information meeting (a)-(d) is free from restrictions, creating a clear in-text contradiction with the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Each party is aware, and will advise its Representatives who are informed of the matters that are the subject of this Agreement, of the restrictions imposed by the United States securities laws on the purchase or sale of securities by any person who has received material, non-public information from the issuer of such securities and on the communication of such information to any other person when it is reasonably foreseeable that such other person is likely to purchase or sell such securities in reliance upon such information.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party is aware, and will advise its Representatives who are informed of the matters that are the subject of this Agreement, of the restrictions imposed by the United States securities laws on the purchase or sale of securities by any person who has received material, non-public information from the issuer of such securities and on the communication of such information to any other person when it is reasonably foreseeable that such other person is likely to purchase or sell such securities in reliance upon such information. **However, this awareness does not create any legal obligation for the receiving party to actively monitor or prevent its Representatives from engaging in such activities, and any actions taken by Representatives in violation of securities laws shall not be attributable to the Receiving Party unless there is direct evidence of the Receiving Party's intent to facilitate such violations.**
perturbation.explanation: This change adds a statement that effectively undermines the warning about insider trading liability. The original sentence implies a responsibility to advise representatives about securities law restrictions. The added sentence suggests that the Receiving Party has no real obligation to prevent insider trading by its representatives. This introduces ambiguity about the Receiving Party's responsibility and weakens the initial warning.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party is aware, and will advise its Representatives who are informed of the matters that are the subject of this Agreement, of the restrictions imposed by the United States securities laws on the purchase or sale of securities by any person who has received material, non-public information from the issuer of such securities and on the communication of such information to any other person when it is reasonably foreseeable that such other person is likely to purchase or sell such securities in reliance upon such information. However, this awareness does not create any legal obligation for the receiving party to actively monitor or prevent its Representatives from engaging in such activities, and any actions taken by Representatives in violation of securities laws shall not be attributable to the Receiving Party unless there is direct evidence of the Receiving Party's intent to facilitate such violations.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause stating that the Receiving Party has no obligation to actively monitor or prevent insider trading by its representatives, effectively negating the responsibility implied by the original sentence in the Contradicted Text which states Each party is aware, and will advise its Representatives...of the restrictions imposed by the United States securities laws. This undermines the initial warning and creates ambiguity about the Receiving Party's true responsibility, leading to a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The provisions herein concerning the disclosure, protection and use of Confidential Information, including Sections 3, 4, 7 and 9, shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement. The non-solicit and standstill obligations shall remain in effect as provided in Sections 13 or 14, as applicable.
perturbation.changed_text: The provisions herein concerning the disclosure, protection and use of Confidential Information, including Sections 3, 4, 7 and 9, shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement. **However, this survival clause does not apply if the Evaluation is unsuccessful, and the parties do not enter into a definitive agreement within six months of the Effective Date; in such case, all confidentiality obligations shall immediately cease.** The non-solicit and standstill obligations shall remain in effect as provided in Sections 13 or 14, as applicable.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the first sentence of section 19 by conditionally removing the survival of the confidentiality provisions under certain conditions. The original states those sections 'shall survive the termination or expiration'. The addition imposes an entirely new exception that invalidates the guaranteed survival of the stated sections if a final agreement isn't reached. The in-text contradiction is located inside of section 19, where the initial sentence explicitly says the mentioned section 'shall survive', while the second sentence says 'However, this survival clause does not apply if...'
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term of this Agreement shall be one (1) year beginning with the Effective Date, unless terminated earlier by either party at such party’s sole discretion upon thirty (30) days written notice to the other party. The provisions herein concerning the disclosure, protection and use of Confidential Information, including Sections 3, 4, 7 and 9, shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement. However, this survival clause does not apply if the Evaluation is unsuccessful, and the parties do not enter into a definitive agreement within six months of the Effective Date; in such case, all confidentiality obligations shall immediately cease. The non-solicit and standstill obligations shall remain in effect as provided in Sections 13 or 14, as applicable.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a condition to the survival clause of the confidentiality provisions. The original text states that sections 3, 4, 7, and 9 'shall survive' termination or expiration. The change introduces an exception: the survival clause doesn't apply if the evaluation is unsuccessful and a definitive agreement isn't reached within six months. This directly contradicts the original 'shall survive' statement, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is within the same section, section 19, where the added condition negates the initial guarantee of survival under specific circumstances, hence "YES".

perturbation.original_text: Without prejudice to the rights and remedies otherwise available to either party hereto, each party hereto shall be entitled to equitable relief by way of injunction or otherwise if the other party or any of its Representatives breach or threaten to breach any of the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Without prejudice to the rights and remedies otherwise available to either party hereto, each party hereto shall be entitled to equitable relief by way of injunction or otherwise if the other party or any of its Representatives breach or threaten to breach any of the provisions of this Agreement. **However, no monetary damages shall be awarded to either party under any circumstances for any breach of this agreement, except in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct.**
perturbation.explanation: This modification introduces an internal contradiction within Section 15. The original clause grants the right to equitable relief (injunction), broadly applying to any breach. However, the added clause restricts remedies by stating that no monetary damages will be awarded for any breach of this agreement, except in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct. This creates ambiguity and uncertainty. If a breach occurs, and equitable relief isn't sufficient, can the damaged party pursue other remedies? The in-text contradiction in section 15, arises due to the contradiction on the remedies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Without prejudice to the rights and remedies otherwise available to either party hereto, each party hereto shall be entitled to equitable relief by way of injunction or otherwise if the other party or any of its Representatives breach or threaten to breach any of the provisions of this Agreement. However, no monetary damages shall be awarded to either party under any circumstances for any breach of this agreement, except in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a strong in-text contradiction. The original text granted the right to equitable relief (injunction) for any breach. The added clause restricts remedies, stating that no monetary damages will be awarded for any breach of this agreement, except in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct. This directly contradicts the previous clause that gave a party rights to remedies if there was a breach. Therefore there is a contradiction.
